author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Canon XVII.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: That any province on account of its distance, may have its own Primate.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: It seemed good that Mauretania Sitiphensis, as it asked, should have a Primate of its own, with the consent of the Primate of Numidia from whose synod it had been separated.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And with the consent of all the primates of the African Provinces and of all the bishops permission was given, by reason of the great distance between them.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Mauretania Sitiphensis, on account of the great distance, is permitted to have its own Primate.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This canon is Canon iij. of the first series of canons enacted at Hippo in 393.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: From this place forward the Latin and Greek numeration varies; but Justellus’s Edition in Greek and Latin follows the Latin division.
